note: Per-drug A0 (no-graph) hit-rate over repeated trials, seed 62, to resolve the clean-3 split in paper15 S6. A0 ignores retrieval (no engine). KEY FINDING: A0 is NOT deterministic even at temp 0 -- it fluctuates ~1 drug per run -- so single-run per-drug counts are unreliable. We therefore report hit-rate over 3 trials x 4 models = 12 samples per drug.
conclusion: Only BPDCN/pivekimab is a genuine out-of-training isolation (A0 ~8%); Hunter (83%) and achondroplasia (50%) are substantially inferable from nomenclature. The paper's original 'A0=0/3 on three clean names' was wrong on two counts (impossible vs A0=84.6%, and 2 of the 3 are inferable). paper15 S6 corrected to robust numbers. A_agent = A_det = 100% (stable across runs).
